---
title: "ggpubr Figures — Yogurt Study"
format: html
editor: source
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

> Visualizing the L. iners comparison across study arms and time points with `ggpubr`.

## Setup

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(ggpubr)

samples <- read_csv(
  here("data/group_a_yogurt/00_sample_ids_yogurt.csv"),
  col_types = cols(pid = col_character())
)
qpcr <- read_csv(
  here("data/group_a_yogurt/02_qpcr_results_yogurt.csv"),
  col_types = cols(sample_id = col_character())
)

df <- samples |>
  select(sample_id, pid, arm, time_point) |>
  inner_join(qpcr, by = "sample_id") |>
  mutate(
    log_iners  = log(qpcr_iners + 1),
    time_point = fct_relevel(time_point, "baseline")
  )
```

## Figure 1 — Boxplots by arm, faceted by time point

```{r fig1}
cols <- c("unchanged_diet" = "#0073C2", "yogurt" = "#EFC000")

ggplot(df, aes(x = arm, y = log_iners, color = arm)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ time_point) +
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = list(c("unchanged_diet", "yogurt")),
    vjust = 0.4
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  theme_pubr() +
  labs(
    title = "L. iners by study arm",
    subtitle = "Faceted by time point",
    x = "Arm",
    y = "log(L. iners + 1)"
  )
```

## Figure 2 — Paired before-after lines by participant

```{r fig2}
ggplot(df, aes(x = time_point, y = log_iners, group = pid, color = arm)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.2, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = arm), alpha = 0.15, outlier.shape = NA,
               color = "grey30") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.08, alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  stat_compare_means(
    aes(group = time_point),
    label = "p.format",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    comparisons = list(c("baseline", "after_antibiotic")),
    tip.length = 0
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ arm) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols) +
  theme_pubr() +
  labs(
    title = "L. iners: baseline → after antibiotic",
    subtitle = "Each line = one participant; paired Wilcoxon p-value",
    x = "Time point",
    y = "log(L. iners + 1)"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

## Summary stats

```{r summary}
df |>
  group_by(arm, time_point) |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median_log = round(median(log_iners), 2),
    q25 = round(quantile(log_iners, 0.25), 2),
    q75 = round(quantile(log_iners, 0.75), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```
